content: For PROC-06, how many steps are fix-before-automate? Answer with the count.

content.content:
{"content": "Subject: OS&D at the dock
STEP-61 the damage/overage note is handwritten and it's a judgment whether it's
short or damaged. STEP-62 the shortage log is a spreadsheet standing in for a
system nobody built. STEP-64 the exception gets routed to a person by email, and
the disposition reason is only in the thread. STEP-66 the carrier claim is filed
from a pile of unstructured notes. (STEP-68 is the returns-to-vendor flow,
different flow - ignore.)", "kind": "email_thread", "process_id": "PROC-06", "source_id": "SRC-601"}

{"kind": "spreadsheet", "process_id": "PROC-06", "rows": [["step_code", "note"], ["STEP-60", "receipt scan/count still half paper at the dock; totals keyed later"], ["STEP-63", "exception qty in eaches but PO in cases - pack math never lines up"]], "source_id": "SRC-602"}
{"content": "Portal path:\nScreen 4 -> STEP-65 post the exception adjustment into the system (structured).", "kind": "portal_clickpath", "process_id": "PROC-06", "source_id": "SRC-603"}

{"content": "order of ops: STEP-60 -> STEP-61 -> STEP-62 -> STEP-63 -> STEP-64 -> STEP-65 ->\nSTEP-66. the handoff from STEP-64 to STEP-65 is literally an email to the desk\nthat keys the adjustment. then you file the claim.", "kind": "tribal_note", "process_id": "PROC-06", "source_id": "SRC-604"}
